articles.title: China Hongqiao targets US$1.5 billion from share sale in Hong Kong amid aluminium boom
articles.summary: China Hongqiao Group, the country's largest private aluminium producer, aims to raise HK$11.68 billion (US$1.5 billion) from a share sale to fund projects and repay debt, capitalising on robust industry margins and a buoyant Hong Kong equity market. The company planned to sell up to 400 million existing shares for HK$29.20 each, representing a 9.6 per cent discount to its closing price on Monday, according to a filing to the Hong Kong stock exchange on Tuesday. articles.title: Chinese aluminium smelter rides Hong Kong IPO wave with US$707 million fundraising
articles.summary: Chinese aluminium smelter Chuangxin Industries Holdings aims to raise as much as HK$5.5 billion (US$707 million) in an initial public offering (IPO) that has already drawn commitments from 18 domestic and international investors, highlighting the strong appetite in Hong Kong's buoyant fundraising market. articles.title: Simandou iron ore project commences operations
articles.summary: The project includes the construction of more than 600km of new multi-use trans-Guinean rail, as well as barge and trans-shipment vessel port facilities.

articles.title: Asian Dividend Stocks To Watch In July 2025
articles.summary: As Asian markets navigate a landscape marked by modest gains in Japan and steady growth in China, investors are increasingly turning their attention to dividend stocks as a potential source of stability and income. In this environment, identifying stocks with strong fundamentals and consistent dividend payouts can be an attractive strategy for those seeking reliable returns amidst broader economic uncertainties.
